governing_requirement: Reconstruct and install exact published release bytes from a clean environment, detect editable/dirty/unpinned/stale installations, verify every installed hash, and atomically restore the exact prior adopted release through a deterministic rollback command.
prerequisites: MF-P6-10.01 must publish the exact byte catalog and structured installation/rollback identities.
MF-P6-10.02 must publish the qualified capability snapshot whose installed state is being adopted.
existing_foundations: The dependency-light ComfyUI node module and workflows can be copied into a temporary ComfyUI tree.
The installer pins the service URL to http://127.0.0.1:8765 and writes deterministic local package-root/ontology configuration.
The copied node pack imports without heavy model dependencies and the focused installer/runtime tests pass.
The frozen release schema declares installer, install-manifest, verification-workflow, rollback, target-release, and verification-evidence identities.

confirmed_gaps.observed: pip reports MaskFactory 0.0.1 as an editable project at C:/Comfy_UI_Main_Masking, and both installer and copied node module resolve directly from that source tree.
confirmed_gaps.impact: The current command cannot distinguish published release bytes from uncommitted source changes.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The stale file remains because copytree uses dirs_exist_ok and no closed installed-file inventory is reconciled.
confirmed_gaps.impact: A stale or malicious prior node can coexist with the nominally installed release.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The installed tree contains __init__.py, config.json, workflows, and the seeded stale file; no install manifest, release identity, per-file hash catalog, verification receipt, or atomic completion marker is written.

install_node_pack mutates the live target in place with mkdir/copy/write operations, takes no prior-release snapshot, exposes no rollback callable or CLI command, and cannot restore an exact previous installed tree after a partial or bad install.

A no-build-isolation wheel probe fails during metadata generation with invalid command bdist_wheel. pyproject build-system requirements are unpinned (setuptools>=68 and wheel), wheel is not pinned in env/requirements.lock.txt or env/maskfactory_env.yml, and the active host has no wheel package.
confirmed_gaps.impact: Using build isolation would fetch unspecified build-tool versions; the repository has no governed command proving two clean builds match published wheel and node-pack hashes.

confirmed_gaps.observed: pyproject package-data declares only schemas/*.json. The failed wheel probe means the workflow JSON inventory has not been proven present and hash-identical in a built distribution.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive release-root installer/verifier/rollback transaction that consumes the adopted 10.01 publication catalog and 10.02 capability snapshot without treating editable source or copied mutable paths as authority.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. They are disjoint from the held MF-P6-10.01 publication paths and the preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty scope. Candidate paths are read-only.
acceptance_criteria: Consume only an exact trusted 10.01 publication decision and adopted 10.02 capability snapshot; resolve every referenced release byte before mutation.
Refuse editable installs, dirty/untracked source trees, unpinned build tools, source-folder node copies, missing release hashes, and incompatible installed runtime identities.
Build wheel and node-pack archives twice in independent clean environments with pinned build tools and require exact catalog/hash equality, including workflow JSON and entry-point inventories.
Stage into a new contained directory, verify the closed file manifest and hashes, fsync where supported, then switch the active release atomically with receipt-last semantics.
Reject extra, missing, changed, case-colliding, symlink/reparse/hardlink-like, path-escaping, or stale files before adoption; no merge-in-place installation.
Persist exact previous/next release identities, install manifest, verification evidence, active pointer, and rollback transaction in a strict self-hashed evidence record.
Rollback restores the exact prior adopted tree and active pointer, re-verifies every hash and node/API inventory, preserves history, and fails closed on prior-state drift.
Kill/fault tests at every staging/switch/receipt boundary leave either the previous verified release active or a recoverable staged transaction, never a mixed tree.
Frozen v1 schemas and fixtures, the held 10.01 unit, queued worker records, and MF-P6-08.05 paths remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit proves the basic developer installer behavior and isolates missing production packaging/install/rollback authority. It does not publish, clean-build, install, adopt, or roll back a MaskFactory release and does not complete MF-P6-10.04.
